headlines.text: Trump in Situation Room making 'final determination' on Iran ceasefire deal as Tehran says no agreement reached yet
US and Iran traded strikes overnight even as negotiators claim a tentative 60-day truce extension is on the table
Gas prices fall 3.3 cents in biggest single-day drop since 2008, hitting $4.39 national average
S&P 500 and Nasdaq closed at record highs Thursday; markets up again Friday on Iran deal hopes and AI rally
Stephen Colbert's Late Show finale aired May 21-22 with Paul McCartney, Jon Stewart and a wormhole — CBS refused to cover it
The Mandalorian and Grogu opens to $98M domestic over Memorial Day weekend — worst Star Wars opening since Solo
Pam Bondi gives closed-door House interview Friday over her handling of the Epstein files
Blue Origin rocket explodes in major setback to Jeff Bezos's spaceflight ambitions
Former CIA officer David Rush accused of stealing tens of millions in gold bars and stashing them at his Virginia home
U.S. inflation hit 3.8% in April, its highest rate since 2023, as Iran war oil shock squeezes consumers

breaking.text: Trump says he's meeting in Situation Room NOW to make 'final determination' on Iran deal — Tehran still denying any agreement

stories.hed: Deal or No Deal: Trump Heads to Situation Room as Iran Says 'What Agreement?'
stories.dek: Negotiators on both sides claim a tentative 60-day ceasefire extension is basically done — except Iran's foreign ministry says the text hasn't been finalized, and Trump hasn't signed anything yet. It's the diplomatic equivalent of both people reaching for the check and then neither one paying.

stories.hed: Iran Fired a Missile Toward Kuwait Even While Peace Talks Were Underway
stories.dek: US Central Command confirmed Iran targeted US forces in Kuwait overnight, with Tehran claiming it was payback for earlier American strikes near Bandar Abbas. Nothing says 'we want a deal' quite like lobbing a ballistic missile at your counterpart's allies mid-negotiation.

stories.hed: Federal Judge Blocks DOJ's $1.8 Billion 'Anti-Weaponization Fund' Created as Part of Trump-IRS Settlement
stories.dek: A Virginia federal judge temporarily blocked the DOJ from spending money from its controversial $1.8 billion fund — created after Trump sued the IRS over his leaked tax records — while a legal challenge proceeds. The fund was meant to compensate alleged victims of Biden-era prosecutorial overreach, which is a sentence that contains many assumptions.

stories.hed: Detainees at Newark's Delaney Hall ICE Facility Allegedly Beaten After Hunger Strike
stories.dek: Relatives of migrants detained at the GEO-run Delaney Hall facility in Newark say incarcerated people were beaten and pepper-sprayed after a hunger strike, while federal officials deny a hunger strike is even happening. This is a normal Friday.

stories.hed: Pentagon Awarded Dell a $9.7 Billion Contract Days After Trump Disclosed His Trust Bought Dell Shares
stories.dek: Trump's trust actively traded Dell stock earlier this month — an unprecedented move for a sitting president — and now Dell has a nearly $10 billion defense software deal. The administration says these things are unrelated, which is exactly what someone would say if they were related.

markets.context: Stocks hit records on AI enthusiasm and Iran deal optimism while oil fell 2% on hopes the Strait of Hormuz may reopen soon.

gas.note: Prices dropped 3 cents Thursday in the biggest single-day decline since 2008 — meaning the Iran war is still costing you roughly $1.50 more per gallon than it did in February, just slightly less so.

popCulture.hed: Stephen Colbert Ends 11-Year Late Show Run With Paul McCartney, a Wormhole, and CBS Refusing to Cover Its Own Finale
popCulture.dek: The May 21-22 series finale drew 6.74 million viewers — the most-watched weeknight episode in the show's history — with McCartney, Jon Stewart, Bryan Cranston, and the entire late-night host gang showing up to say goodbye. CBS Morning refused to cover it, Trump celebrated on Truth Social at 2am, and Colbert is now apparently writing a Lord of the Rings movie with his son.

genZ.definition: TikTok creators are building absurdist fantasy squads for the 2026 World Cup by drafting pop culture icons, politicians, and random inanimate objects instead of actual footballers — a Bosnian creator put a can of chicken pate on the roster and it went massively viral. With the real World Cup kicking off in June and Iran's team still unable to get US visas, the meme has taken on a surreal geopolitical layer.
genZ.usage: Trump's Iran deal negotiating team, ranked by World Cup position: Bessent at striker (threatens Oman for fun), Vance at midfielder (says 'not there yet' every 20 minutes), and a can of chicken pate in goal.

tldr: Trump spent Friday in the Situation Room weighing a tentative US-Iran ceasefire deal that Iran says doesn't exist yet, while oil prices fell 2% on deal hopes and gas finally got a little cheaper for the first time in months. Meanwhile the Late Show is gone, Baby Yoda underperformed at the box office, and a former CIA officer allegedly hid millions in gold bars in his Virginia house, which is somehow not the most unbelievable story of the day.
